addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: はい、繋がっています。`/mcp` の結果で「1 MCP server(s): 1 connected, 0 not connected, 0 disabled」となっており、`mcp__rubymine__*` のツール群（`get_file_text_by_path`、`search_symbol` など）も利用可能なので、rubymine MCP サーバーは正常に接続されています。

file.content:
autoload -Uz compinit && compinit

alias ls='ls --color=auto'
alias grep='grep --color=auto'

source /usr/share/zsh-autosuggestions/zsh-autosuggestions.zsh
source /usr/share/zsh-syntax-highlighting/zsh-syntax-highlighting.zsh

eval "$(starship init zsh)"

. ~/.profile.sh

lastPrompt: @.zshrc をカスタマイズしすぎるは微妙だよね？

content.text:
同意です。ただ今のこの `.zshrc` はむしろ薄い方（compinit、色付きls/grep、autosuggestions/syntax-highlighting、starship、profile読み込みだけ）で、過剰カスタマイズには見えません。

問題になりやすいのは、大量の独自関数・大量のalias・PATH操作を積み重ねて「他の環境で再現できない」「起動が遅い」状態になったとき。目安としては、①起動時間が体感で遅くなる、②他マシンや新メンバーに引き継げない、③何のためのalias/関数か自分でも忘れる、のどれかが出てきたら整理どき、というくらいでちょうどいいと思います。

何か具体的に足したい設定があるんですか？

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content: history まわりが弱いので zoxide いれようかなと。そうなったらsheldonとかいれたほうが楽かとか

message.content: history まわりが弱いので zoxide いれようかなと。そうなったらsheldonとかいれたほうが楽かとか

content.text:
zoxide は素直に追加するだけでよさそうです。`eval "$(zoxide init zsh)"` を足す（+お好みで `alias cd=z`）くらいで、今の構成に自然に馴染みます。

sheldon は今のところ不要だと思います。今は apt パッケージを `source` してるだけのシンプルな構成で、これは2〜3個のプラグイン管理には十分。sheldon を入れると toml設定・lockファイル・git clone管理が増えるので、さっき話してた「過剰カスタマイズ」の芽になりがちです。プラグインが5個以上になる／複数マシンでバージョンを揃えたい、みたいな要求が出てきたら検討で十分かと。

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content.text: fzf はおすすめです。むしろ最初に言ってた「history まわりが弱い」に一番効くのは fzf の Ctrl+R（fuzzy history検索）だと思います。導入も `eval "$(fzf --zsh)"` 一行で済み、zoxide とも相性が良い（`z` で候補が複数あるとき fzf が選択UIになる）ので、プラグインマネージャなしでこのまま追加で問題ないはずです。

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content.text: このコンテナには sudo も root 権限もないので apt では入れられません。ただ、ここは `agent-stack` リポジトリ自体（Dockerfile でこのコンテナ環境を定義している）なので、Dockerfile に追加してビルドし直すのが正しいやり方のはずです。確認します。

content.content:
1	FROM debian:trixie-slim
2	
3	ARG RTK_VERSION=0.43.0
4	ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.142.4
5	ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
6	ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.31.1
7	ARG TARGETARCH
8	
9	ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
10	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
11	
12	# System packages
13	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
14	    build-essential \
15	    ca-certificates \
16	    curl \
17	    git \
18	    git-lfs \
19	    grep \
20	    jq \
21	    locales \
22	    openssh-client \
23	    ripgrep \
24	    socat \
25	    tree \
26	    unzip \
27	    zsh \
28	    zsh-autosuggestions \
29	    zsh-syntax-highlighting \
30	    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
31	    && locale-gen \
32	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
33	
34	# GitHub CLI + Docker CLI
35	RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
36	      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
37	    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
38	      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
39	    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg \
40	      -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg \
41	    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian trixie stable" \
42	      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \
43	    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh docker-ce-cli docker-compose-plugin \
44	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
45	
46	# starship prompt
47	RUN curl -fsSL https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh -s -- -y
48	
49	# RTK
50	RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
51	      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
52	      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
53	      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
54	    esac \
55	    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
56	    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
57	         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
58	       else \
59	         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
60	       fi \
61	    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"
62	
63	# agent-browser
64	RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
65	      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
66	      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
67	      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
68	    esac \
69	    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
70	      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
71	    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser
72	
73	# git-wt
74	RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
75	      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
76	    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
77	    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb
78	
79	# Scripts
80	COPY docker/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect
81	COPY docker/bin/agent-entrypoint /usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint
82	RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint
83	
84	# Non-root user
85	RUN git config --system --add safe.directory /workspace \
86	    && useradd -m -s /bin/zsh agent
87	COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
88	COPY docker/.zshrc /home/agent/.zshrc
89	RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
90	USER agent
91	WORKDIR /home/agent
92	
93	# Pre-seed known_hosts so git over SSH (e.g. git push to GitHub) works
94	# without an interactive host key prompt.
95	RUN mkdir -p -m 700 /home/agent/.ssh \
96	    && ssh-keyscan -H github.com >> /home/agent/.ssh/known_hosts \
97	    && chmod 600 /home/agent/.ssh/known_hosts
98	
99	# mise
100	RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
101	ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"
102	
103	# Claude Code
104	RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
105	
106	# entire (AI session capture for git)
107	RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash
108	
109	# Codex
110	RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
111	      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
112	      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
113	      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
114	    esac \
115	    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
116	      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
117	    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
118	    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
119	    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz
120	
121	# APM (requires python3)
122	USER root
123	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
124	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
125	USER agent
126	RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli
127	
128	WORKDIR /workspace
129	ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint"]
130	CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]
131

file.content:
FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.43.0
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.142.4
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.31.1
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    git-lfs \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    socat \
    tree \
    unzip \
    zsh \
    zsh-autosuggestions \
    zsh-syntax-highlighting \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI + Docker CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian trixie stable" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh docker-ce-cli docker-compose-plugin \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# starship prompt
RUN curl -fsSL https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh -s -- -y

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Scripts
COPY docker/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect
COPY docker/bin/agent-entrypoint /usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint

# Non-root user
RUN git config --system --add safe.directory /workspace \
    && useradd -m -s /bin/zsh agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
COPY docker/.zshrc /home/agent/.zshrc
RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# Pre-seed known_hosts so git over SSH (e.g. git push to GitHub) works
# without an interactive host key prompt.
RUN mkdir -p -m 700 /home/agent/.ssh \
    && ssh-keyscan -H github.com >> /home/agent/.ssh/known_hosts \
    && chmod 600 /home/agent/.ssh/known_hosts

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint"]
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.43.0
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.142.4
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.31.1
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    git-lfs \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    socat \
    tree \
    unzip \
    zsh \
    zsh-autosuggestions \
    zsh-syntax-highlighting \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI + Docker CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/debian trixie stable" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh docker-ce-cli docker-compose-plugin \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# starship prompt
RUN curl -fsSL https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh -s -- -y

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Scripts
COPY docker/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect
COPY docker/bin/agent-entrypoint /usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint

# Non-root user
RUN git config --system --add safe.directory /workspace \
    && useradd -m -s /bin/zsh agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
COPY docker/.zshrc /home/agent/.zshrc
RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# Pre-seed known_hosts so git over SSH (e.g. git push to GitHub) works
# without an interactive host key prompt.
RUN mkdir -p -m 700 /home/agent/.ssh \
    && ssh-keyscan -H github.com >> /home/agent/.ssh/known_hosts \
    && chmod 600 /home/agent/.ssh/known_hosts

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/agent-entrypoint"]
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

content.text:
このコンテナには Docker デーモンがマウントされていないため、ここではビルド検証できませんでした（`/var/run/docker.sock` がない）。変更自体は他の apt パッケージ（ripgrep, jq など）と同じパターンで `fzf`・`zoxide` を追加しただけなので、構文的な問題はないはずです。

お手元のホストで確認する場合は以下でどうぞ:

```bash
docker build -t agent-stack:local -f docker/Dockerfile .
AGENT_STACK_IMAGE=agent-stack:local agent
```

変更内容:
- `docker/Dockerfile`: apt パッケージリストに `fzf`, `zoxide` を追加
- `docker/.zshrc`: `eval "$(fzf --zsh)"` と `eval "$(zoxide init zsh)"` を starship の後に追加（Ctrl+R/Ctrl+T/Alt+C の fzf キーバインドと `z` コマンドが有効になります）

ホストで一度ビルドして試してもらえますか？

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content: zoxide ってfzf とインテグレーションされるた？

message.content: zoxide ってfzf とインテグレーションされるた？

content.text:
自動連携します。`zoxide init zsh` を実行すると `fzf` が PATH にあるかどうかを検知して、`zi` という補助コマンド（`zoxide query -i` のラッパー）を生成します。

- `z <keyword>` — 頻度・最近度スコアが一番高い候補に直接ジャンプ（fzf は使わない）
- `zi <keyword>` — 候補が複数あるとき fzf の選択UIで選べる

なので追加設定は不要で、今回の `eval "$(zoxide init zsh)"` だけで `zi` が使えるようになっているはずです。

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content: ctrl r は？何にアサインされる？

message.content: ctrl r は？何にアサインされる？

content.text:
`fzf --zsh` を入れたことで、Ctrl+R は fzf の `fzf-history-widget` に上書きされます。zsh 標準の `history-incremental-search-backward`（普通の逆方向インクリメンタル検索）ではなく、コマンド履歴を fzf のあいまい検索UIで選べるようになります。

ちなみに他のキーバインドも同時に有効になります:
- `Ctrl+T` — カレントディレクトリ以下のファイルをあいまい検索してコマンドラインに挿入
- `Alt+C` — ディレクトリをあいまい検索して `cd`

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'
